channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns About the Future of Professional Journalism
item.description: The article reflects on the state of professional journalism and its challenges in today’s media landscape. The author, Noel Wauchope, emphasizes that while all journalism carries some bias, transpare...

item.title: Trump criticizes media coverage of Iran war, calling reports false and harmful
item.description: On May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social accusing media outlets of committing 'virtual treason' for reporting that Iran is performing well in the ongoing war. Trump stated that s...

item.title: Ben Shahn’s 1938 Photographs Document Daily Life in Ohio
item.description: In 1938, renowned photographer Ben Shahn traveled across Central Ohio for the Farm Security Administration (FSA) to document the lives of residents during the final years of the Great Depression. His ...

item.title: South C Residents Demand Update on Investigation Into Deadly Building Collapse
item.description: Residents of South C in Nairobi have given Lands, Housing and Urban Development Cabinet Secretary Alice Wahome a seven-day deadline to provide updates on the probe into a 16-storey building that colla...

item.title: Anand Patwardhan on Documenting Social and Political Struggles Through Cinema
item.description: The G5A cultural centre in Mumbai is hosting a retrospective of Anand Patwardhan’s work from October 2 to 5, showcasing 10 of his 13 documentaries and two short films. Over five decades, the 75-year-o...

item.title: BC Nurses Approve Strike Amid Contract Negotiation Stalemate
item.description: Members of the British Columbia Nurses' Union have overwhelmingly voted in favour of strike action, with 98.2 per cent supporting it. The decision comes after stalled negotiations with the Health Empl...

item.title: Ethiopia’s Position in Sudan Conflict Raises Regional Concerns
item.description: For long time now, Sudan war been ongoing between Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and Rapid Support Forces (RSF), with foreign countries secretly backing different sides. But recently, situation change as...

item.title: How Supertramp’s 'Breakfast in America' Album Cover Was Created
item.description: Supertramp's 1979 album 'Breakfast in America' features one of the most inventive and iconic album covers of the 1970s. Designed to resemble a view of Lower Manhattan from an airplane window, the cove...

item.title: The 1948 Healey Westland Roadster: British Engineering and Design Excellence
item.description: The 1948 Healey 2.4-Litre Westland Roadster represents a remarkable achievement in post-war British automotive engineering. Designed by Donald Healey, this hand-built sports car was among the fastest ...

item.title: Zanzibar launches recruitment to ease health worker shortage
item.description: Zanzibar government has announced new employment opportunities in the health sector to address the ongoing shortage of medical staff across the islands. The initiative targets doctors, nurses, and oth...

item.title: Kelvin Evans jailed for stealing Beyoncé’s unreleased music in Atlanta

item.title: City of Swan to fund independent review of planning department following community concerns
item.description: The City of Swan council in Perth is preparing to allocate $50,000 for an independent review of its planning department after several residents raised concerns about how planning issues have been hand...

item.title: Federal Budget 2026: Major Tax and Housing Reforms Aim to Help Australians Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: Australia’s 2026 federal budget, presented by Treasurer Jim Chalmers, introduces the most significant tax reforms since the Howard era, including changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax ...

item.title: Trial of Bafana Mahungela Over Kirsten Kluyts' Death Scheduled in Johannesburg
item.description: The Johannesburg High Court is set to hear the murder trial of 23-year-old Bafana Mahungela, accused of killing teacher Kirsten Kluyts, who was pregnant at the time of her death in October 2023. Mahun...

item.title: Promising Ilocos Norte Teen Runner Passes Away at 18
item.description: Mark Dave Nicolas, an 18-year-old track sensation from Ilocos Norte, was seen as one of the Philippines' brightest young athletes. Nicknamed the province's next big sports star, he stunned the nation ...

item.title: Trump defends expansion of White House ballroom amid rising costs
item.description: During a press gaggle outside the White House on May 12, 2026, President Donald Trump reacted angrily when questioned about the escalating costs of a planned $1 billion ballroom at the White House. He...

item.title: TADB Introduces Strategy to Support Climate-Friendly Farming 2025–2027
item.description: The Tanzania Agricultural Development Bank (TADB) has officially rolled out its Green Agri-Finance Strategy for the period 2025–2027. This initiative is designed to promote climate-smart farming acros...

item.title: Khan Sir comments on NEET exam paper leak situation
item.description: Khan Sir, a popular educator known for his candid opinions on educational issues, recently addressed the ongoing controversy surrounding the alleged NEET paper leak. According to sources, he emphasize...

item.title: Father Adit Deng launches legal action against Victoria over daughter's death
item.description: Adit Deng, grieving father of Aluel Ajaks, has initiated legal proceedings against the Victorian government, seeking accountability and justice for his daughter's tragic death. The lawsuit reportedly ...

item.title: UN Chief Praises Africa’s Role in Global Leadership and Calls for Fair Partnerships
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres delivered a keynote at the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, highlighting Africa's increasing role in global decision-making. He stressed that Africa is not waiti...
